revision.comment: 上之村神社の項目の作成
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 *[[事代主命]] *[[大山祇神]] *[[大己貴命]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を改称した際に、主祭神を[[大山祇神]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社 == *大雷神社 [[別雷神]]を祭神とし、「上之の雷電さま」と周辺の住民から親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。 大雷神社の造営に際し、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている [https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' *[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 '''交通アクセス''' *熊谷市内循環バス「池上」下車　徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 *[[事代主命]] *[[大山祇神]] *[[大己貴命]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を改称した際に、主祭神を[[大山祇神]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社 == *大雷神社 [[別雷神]]を祭神とし、「上之の雷電さま」と周辺の住民から親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。 大雷神社の造営に際し、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている [https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' *[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 '''交通アクセス''' *熊谷市内循環バス「池上」下車　徒歩約10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}

[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[大己貴命]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社 == [[別雷神]]を祭神とし、「上之の雷電さま」と周辺の住民から親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。 大雷神社の造営に際し、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている [https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 * 大雷神社 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[熊谷市内循環バス]]「池上」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩で約10分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|thumb|240px]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。 本社、摂社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地で12代目五郎家時は600年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社　上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[大己貴命]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。 例祭　7月27日 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社　大雷神社（だいらいじんじゃ） == *祭神…[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ） 埼玉県で最大の雷電神社。およそ1,000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]] (兵庫県豊岡市)より勧請。「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。 ※地域住民は神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」というとわからないほど、雷電神社、という社名が一般化している。 *例祭　7/28 『大雷講（だいらいこう）』 年に一度、この日に限り、宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内（みかきうち）への参入を許される。 関東一円より、雷除け、あるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている [https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[熊谷市内循環バス]]「池上」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩で約10分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之村神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|thumb|240px]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。 本社、摂社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地で12代目五郎家時は600年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社　上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] * [[大己貴命]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。 例祭　7月27日 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社　大雷神社（だいらいじんじゃ） == *祭神…[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ） 埼玉県で最大の雷電神社。およそ1,000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]] (兵庫県豊岡市)より勧請。「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。 ※地域住民は神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」というとわからないほど、雷電神社、という社名が一般化している。 　 *例祭　7/28 『大雷講（だいらいこう）』 年に一度、この日に限り、講員は宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内（みかきうち）への参入を許される。 * 『電気の神様』 関東一円より、雷除け、あるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている [https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[熊谷市内循環バス]]「池上」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩で約10分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之村神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。本社、摂社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地である「上之」に鎮座。成田氏8代目五郎家時は800年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社　上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。例祭は7月27日に行われる。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社　大雷神社（だいらいじんじゃ） == 祭神は[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ）で埼玉県で最大の雷電神社である。およそ1000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]]（兵庫県豊岡市）より勧請した。大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 現在も「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。地域住民には神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」ではなく、雷電神社という社名が一般化している。電気の神様として、関東一円より、雷除けあるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 7月28日に例祭「大雷講（だいらいこう）」が行われる。この日に限り、講員は宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内への参入を許される。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[熊谷市内循環バス]]「池上」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩で約10分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之村神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。本社、摂社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地である「上之」に鎮座。成田氏8代目五郎家時は800年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社　上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。例祭は7月27日に行われる。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社　大雷神社（だいらいじんじゃ） == 祭神は[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ）で埼玉県で最大の雷電神社である。およそ1000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]]（兵庫県豊岡市）より勧請した。大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 現在も「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。地域住民には神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」ではなく、雷電神社という社名が一般化している。電気の神様として、関東一円より、雷除けあるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 7月28日に例祭「大雷講（だいらいこう）」が行われる。この日に限り、講員は宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内への参入を許される。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[熊谷市内循環バス]]「池上」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩で約10分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之村神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

revision.comment: 鎮座地域では「上之村神社」という呼称はせず、もっぱら雷電神社、雷電さまとして親しまれており、近くの国道交差点名も「上之雷電神社」と称しています。

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社'''（かみのむらじんじゃ）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 通称、「上之 雷電神社」「雷電さま」。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。本社、摂社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地である「上之」に鎮座。成田氏8代目五郎家時は800年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社　上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。例祭は7月27日に行われる。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社　大雷神社（だいらいじんじゃ） == 祭神は[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ）で埼玉県で最大の雷電神社である。およそ1000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]]（兵庫県豊岡市）より勧請した。大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 現在も「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。地域住民には神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」ではなく、雷電神社という社名が一般化している。電気の神様として、関東一円より、雷除けあるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 7月28日に例祭「大雷講（だいらいこう）」が行われる。この日に限り、講員は宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内への参入を許される。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[国道17号]][[熊谷バイパス]]「上之（雷電神社）」交差点から、車で1分 * [[ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|熊谷市内循環バスゆうゆうバス]]グライダー号「上之」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約6分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之村神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kaminomura Shrine |Image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == Location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之村神社（奥右）大雷神社（奥左）]] [[File:熊谷市最古の木製鳥居（熊谷市指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之雷電神社両部鳥居]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿付扉一組（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|永禄元年（1558年）に成田長泰寄進による本殿御扉]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 =県社 |創建 = |宮司 =川端康稔 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 =7月27日、7月28日（大雷講） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之雷電神社（上之村（かみのむら）神社・大雷（だいらい）神社'''）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 通称、「上之 雷電神社」「雷電さま」。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。本社、摂社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地である「上之」に鎮座。成田氏8代目五郎家時は800年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社　上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。例祭は7月27日に行われる。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社　大雷神社（だいらいじんじゃ） == 祭神は[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ）で埼玉県で最大の雷電神社である。およそ1000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]]（兵庫県豊岡市）より勧請した。大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 現在も「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。地域住民には神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」ではなく、雷電神社という社名が一般化している。電気の神様として、関東一円より、雷除けあるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 7月28日に例祭「大雷講（だいらいこう）」が行われる。この日に限り、講員は宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内への参入を許される。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[国道17号]][[熊谷バイパス]]「上之（雷電神社）」交差点から、車で1分 * [[ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|熊谷市内循環バスゆうゆうバス]]グライダー号「上之」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約6分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之村神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之村神社（奥右）大雷神社（奥左）]] [[File:熊谷市最古の木製鳥居（熊谷市指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之雷電神社両部鳥居]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿付扉一組（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|永禄元年（1558年）に成田長泰寄進による本殿御扉]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]

revision.comment: deciding to rename this category to a more descriptive one
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kaminomura Shrine |Image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == Location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kaminomura Shrine |Image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}

However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == Location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:上之村神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kaminomura Shrine |Image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == Location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之村神社（奥右）大雷神社（奥左）]] [[File:熊谷市最古の木製鳥居（熊谷市指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之雷電神社両部鳥居]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿付扉一組（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|永禄元年（1558年）に成田長泰寄進による本殿御扉]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 =県社 |創建 = |宮司 =川端康稔 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 =7月27日、7月28日（大雷講） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之雷電神社（上之村（かみのむら）神社・大雷（だいらい）神社'''）は[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。 通称、「上之 雷電神社」「雷電さま」。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。本社、摂社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地である「上之」に鎮座。成田氏8代目五郎家時は800年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社 上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。例祭は7月27日に行われる。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 摂社 大雷神社 (だいらいじんじゃ) == 祭神は[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ）で埼玉県で最大の雷電神社である。およそ1000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]]（兵庫県豊岡市）より勧請した。大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 現在も「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。地域住民には神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」ではなく、雷電神社という社名が一般化している。電気の神様として、関東一円より、雷除けあるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 7月28日に例祭「大雷講（だいらいこう）」が行われる。この日に限り、講員は宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内への参入を許される。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[国道17号]][[熊谷バイパス]]「上之（雷電神社）」交差点から、車で1分 * [[ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|熊谷市内循環バスゆうゆうバス]]グライダー号「上之」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約6分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之雷電神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

revision.comment: 上之村神社の名称と記述を、大雷神社との関係から、修正。ホームページによると、大雷神社は摂社ではなく末社であり、修正。両社にそれぞれアンカーリンクできるように、タイトルを修正。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 上之村神社 |画像 = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之村神社（奥右）大雷神社（奥左）]] [[File:熊谷市最古の木製鳥居（熊谷市指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|上之雷電神社両部鳥居]] [[File:上之雷電神社本殿付扉一組（埼玉県指定文化財）.jpg|thumb|永禄元年（1558年）に成田長泰寄進による本殿御扉]] |所在地 = 埼玉県熊谷市上之16 |ISO = JP-11 |緯度度 = 36 |緯度分 = 9 |緯度秒 = 22.18 |経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 |経度秒 = 57.08 |祭神 = [[事代主命]]
[[大山祇神]]
[[大己貴命]] |神体 = |社格 =県社 |創建 = |宮司 =川端康稔 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]]、銅板ぶき |別名 = らいでんさま |札所等 = |例祭 =7月27日、7月28日（大雷講） |神事 = |地図 = Japan Saitama |ラベル位置 = top }} '''上之村神社''' (かみのむらじんじゃ) は、[[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]の上之地区にある神社。別称は'''上之雷電神社''' (かみのらいでんじんじゃ）。末社と併せて'''上之村神社・大雷神社''' (かみのむらじんじゃ・だいらいじんじゃ）とも呼ばれる。 境内地の広さは、熊谷市内の神社では随一の広さを誇る。本社、末社ともに本殿は県の文化財、また境内入口の木製両部鳥居は市内最古で市の文化財に指定されている。 [[忍城]]主、[[成田氏|成田家]]の本拠地である「上之」に鎮座。成田氏8代目五郎家時は800年以上前に雷電神社を改修し、中興の祖としてしられる。 == 本社 上之村神社 == 祭神は次の3柱『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 * [[大己貴命]] * [[事代主命]] * [[大山祇神]] 江戸期の祭神は[[大山祇神]]のみであったが、明治2年に社名を「久伊豆神社」から「上之村神社」に改称した際に、主祭神を[[事代主命]]、相殿の神を[[大山祇神]]と[[大己貴命]]に改めた。例祭は7月27日に行われる。 == 歴史 == 創建時期ははっきりとしていないが、社伝によると、再建は[[応永]]年間に成田五郎家時によってなされたといわれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。その後、成田氏の庇護の下、有力な神社になっていくが、[[天正]]18年に、成田氏の忍城が落城し、当社は成田氏の庇護から離れ、徳川氏の支配に組み込まれることになった。[[慶長]]9年には、[[徳川家康]]により上之村内30石が安堵されている。社名は、[[明治]]2年に現在の上之村神社に改められるまで、「久伊豆神社」あるいは「久伊豆明神社」と号していた『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31。 == 末社 大雷神社 == 末社の'''大雷神社''' (だいらいじんじゃ）は、[[雷神|大雷神]]（おおいかづちのかみ）を祭神とし、埼玉県で最大の雷電神社である。およそ1000年前に[[雷神社 (豊岡市)|雷神社]]（兵庫県豊岡市）より勧請した。大雷神社の造営に際し、『[[のぼうの城]]』で知られる忍城主、[[成田長泰]]が扉を奉納しており、この扉と大雷神社の本殿は、上之村神社の本殿と共に埼玉県指定文化財となっている[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）。 現在も「上之の雷電さま」と親しまれている『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89。地域住民には神社の正式神社である「上之村神社」ではなく、雷電神社という社名が一般化している。電気の神様として、関東一円より、雷除けあるいは電力会社、電気工事、電化製品、IT事業者からの崇敬を集める。 7月28日に例祭「大雷講（だいらいこう）」が行われる。この日に限り、講員は宮司はじめ神職のみしか参入を許されない御垣内への参入を許される。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[埼玉県]][[熊谷市]]上之16 ; 交通アクセス * [[国道17号]][[熊谷バイパス]]「上之（雷電神社）」交差点から、車で1分 * [[ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|熊谷市内循環バスゆうゆうバス]]グライダー号「上之」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約6分。 ==外部リンク == ・[https://www.kaminomura.me/ 上之雷電神社公式HP] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:かみのむらしんしや}} [[Category:熊谷市の歴史]] [[Category:熊谷市の建築物]] [[Category:埼玉県の神社]]

revision.comment: 神社の正式神社である -> 神社の正式名称である

{{Shrine |Name = Kaminomura Shrine |image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kaminomura Shrine |image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

{{Shrine |Name = Kaminomura Shrine |image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}

{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kumagaya]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings in Kumagaya]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Kaminomura Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:上之村神社]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}

It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shrine |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg|240px]] |Address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|ja|忍城}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei era|ja|応永}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|ja|徳川家康}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|ja|のぼうの城}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|ja|熊谷市}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}}
