However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}}

It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:上之村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg |address = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of [[:en:Oshi Castle|Oshi Castle]]. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:上之村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg |address = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of [[:en:Oshi Castle|Oshi Castle]]. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of [[:en:Oshi Castle|Oshi Castle]]. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] }} [[ja:上之村神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg |address = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of [[:en:Oshi Castle|Oshi Castle]]. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:上之村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:上之村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q59282633]] Wikidata name: Kaminomura-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:58, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg |address = [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]], copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of [[:en:Oshi Castle|Oshi Castle]]. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the [[:en:Ōei|Ōei era]] by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), [[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]] granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''[[:en:The Floating Castle|The Floating Castle]],'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], [[:en:Kumagaya|Kumagaya]], Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上之村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei era}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|en|The Floating Castle|WD=Q11275227|lt=The Floating Castle}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上之村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg |address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|en|Oshi Castle|WD=Q1074393|lt=Oshi Castle}}. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|en|Oshi Castle|WD=Q1074393|lt=Oshi Castle}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei era}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Ikazuchi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|en|The Floating Castle|WD=Q11275227|lt=The Floating Castle}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上之村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|en|Oshi Castle|WD=Q1074393|lt=Oshi Castle}}. [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] [[ja:上之村神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg |address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|1=Kumagaya}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495689}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|en|Oshi Castle|WD=Q1074393|lt=Oshi Castle|1=Oshi Castle}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei era|1=Ōei}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|en|The Floating Castle|WD=Q11275227|lt=The Floating Castle|1=The Floating Castle}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|WD=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上之村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:上之村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q59282633}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kaminomura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kaminomura Shrine.jpg |address = {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 |ISO = JP-11 |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 9 |Latitude seconds = 22.18 |Longitude degrees = 139 |Longitude minutes = 24 |Longitude seconds = 57.08 |Deities = {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |Sacred object = |Shrine Rank = |Established = |Chief priest = |Main hall = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |Alias = Raiden-sama |Pilgrimage = |Festivals = |Rituals = |Map = Japan Saitama |Label Position = top |location = {{ill|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|1=Kumagaya}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.156166666666664|N|139.4158611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Kaminomura Shrine''' (上之村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in the Kamino district of {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. It is popularly known as "Kamino Raiden Shrine" or "Raiden-sama." The shrine boasts the largest precinct area among all shrines in Kumagaya City. Both the main shrine and its auxiliary shrines are designated cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture. Additionally, the wooden Rōbu torii (両部鳥居) at the shrine's entrance is the oldest in the city and has been designated as a municipal cultural property. The shrine is located in "Kamino," the historic stronghold of the {{ill|Narita clan|ja|成田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita clan|1=Narita clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495689}}, lords of {{ill|Oshi Castle|en|Oshi Castle|qid=Q1074393|lt=Oshi Castle|1=Oshi Castle}}. Goro Ietoki Narita, the eighth head of the Narita clan, is credited with reconstructing Kaminomura Shrine over 800 years ago and is revered as its restorer. == Main Shrine: Kaminomura Shrine == The shrine enshrines the following three deities『埼玉の神社　大里　北葛飾　比企』（埼玉県神社庁　1992）p28-31: * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} During the Edo period, only {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} was worshipped as the primary deity. In 1869 (Meiji 2), when the shrine's name was changed from "Kuisu Shrine" (久伊豆神社) to "Kaminomura Shrine," the main deity was designated as {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, with {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} as secondary deities. The annual festival is held on July 27. == History == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unclear, but according to shrine tradition, it was reconstructed during the {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|qid=Q1193078|lt=Ōei era|1=Ōei}} by Goro Ietoki Narita『熊谷の歴史を彩る　史跡・文化財・人物』（熊谷市立熊谷図書館　2011）p88-89. Under the protection of the Narita clan, the shrine grew into a significant local institution. However, in 1590 (Tenshō 18), the fall of Oshi Castle led to the loss of Narita clan patronage, and the shrine came under the control of the Tokugawa clan. In 1604 (Keichō 9), {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted the shrine 30 koku of land within Kamino Village. The name was officially changed to Kaminomura Shrine in 1869 (Meiji 2), having previously been called "Kuisu Shrine" or "Kuisu Myōjin Shrine." == Auxiliary Shrine: Dai Raiden Shrine == The auxiliary shrine enshrines the deity {{ill|Raijin|ja|大雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raijin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ōikazuchi no Kami) and is the largest Raiden Shrine in Saitama Prefecture. It was established roughly 1,000 years ago, with its deity transferred from {{ill|Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|ja|雷神社 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raiden Shrine (Toyooka)|1=Raijin Shrine (Toyooka)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21655264}} in Toyooka, Hyōgo Prefecture. During its construction, {{ill|Narita Nagayasu|ja|成田長泰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narita Nagayasu|1=Narita Nagayasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11495749}}, lord of Oshi Castle and known from the novel ''{{ill|The Floating Castle|en|The Floating Castle|qid=Q11275227|lt=The Floating Castle|1=The Floating Castle}},'' donated the shrine doors. These doors, along with the main halls of both Dai Raiden Shrine and Kaminomura Shrine, are designated as cultural properties of Saitama Prefecture[https://www.pref.saitama.lg.jp/f2216/bunkazai-kensu.html 埼玉県内の国・県指定等文化財件数]（埼玉県ホームページ）. The shrine remains affectionately known as "Raiden-sama of Kamino". Locally, it is more commonly referred to as Raiden Shrine than by its official name, Kaminomura Shrine. As a deity of electricity, the shrine attracts worshippers from across the Kanto region, including power companies, electrical contractors, electronics manufacturers, and IT businesses. The annual festival, "Dairaikō" (大雷講), is held on July 28. On this day, shrine members are granted special permission to enter the sacred inner precinct (御垣内), a privilege typically reserved for priests. == location == * {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kumagaya|en|Kumagaya|qid=Q41106|lt=Kumagaya|1=Kumagaya}}, Kamino 16 == Access == * By car: 1 minute from the "Kamino (Raiden Shrine)" intersection on National Route 17 (Kumagaya Bypass) * By bus: 6-minute walk from the "Kamino" stop on the {{ill|Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|ja|ゆうゆうバス (熊谷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagaya City Yūyū Bus|1=Kumagaya City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11280693}} Glider route == External Links == * [https://www.kaminomura.me/ Official website of Kaminomura Shrine] == References == {{Reflist}} {{translated page|ja|上之村神社|version=103310853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q59282633|ja|上之村神社}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kumagaya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
